slides.title: Angular Masterclass
slides.subtitle:
Modern Enterprise Web Application Development with Angular
Standalone Architecture, RxJS, Signals, Dependency Injection, and Routing

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Software Developer & IoT Enthusiast
slides.oss_experience: Full-Stack Development, Architecture Design & Frontend Engineering Specialist

slides.title: Introduction to Angular
slides.subtitle: Overview of Google's Enterprise-Grade Frontend Framework

slides.title: What is Angular?
slides.imageAlt: Workspace representing development team working on web interfaces
slides.caption: A batteries-included framework for scalable development
slides.content:
рҹӣЎпёҸ Enterprise-Grade Web Platform

Angular is a development platform and framework built on TypeScript for creating highly scalable, single-page web applications (SPAs).

  • рҹ“Ұ Batteries-Included - Official solutions for routing, state management, HTTP requests, form handling, and testing.
  • вҡҷпёҸ Component-Driven - Focuses on modular, reusable pieces of user interfaces.
  • рҹ’Ә TypeScript Native - Strong typing, static analysis, and robust tooling support from day one.

slides.title: Angular vs. React & Vue
leftPoints: рҹ”Ң Bring Your Own Library - Routing, form validation, and state must be chosen from community packages.
рҹ§© Fast, lightweight core that focuses on the view layer.
вңҚпёҸ JSX/Virtual DOM for rendering templates.
рҹҢҖ High flexibility, which can lead to inconsistencies across projects.
rightPoints: рҹ”Ӣ Full Ecosystem - Official router, HTTP client, forms, and validation libraries are built-in.
рҹҸ—пёҸ Opinionated Architecture ensures code looks consistent across teams.
вҡЎ Ahead-Of-Time (AOT) Ivy compiler outputs direct DOM instructions.
рҹӣ пёҸ Standard CLI handles code generation, builds, and updates automatically.

note.text: Angular's opinionated nature drastically simplifies onboarding and maintainability in large enterprises.

box.title: вҸі Evolution from AngularJS to Modern Angular
list: AngularJS (1.x - 2010): The original framework based on MVC and two-way binding. Suffered from scaling and performance limitations.
Angular 2 (2016): A complete rewrite. Shifted to component architecture, TypeScript, and unidirectional data flow.
Ivy Compiler (v9 - 2020): Next-generation compiler that decreased bundle sizes and improved debugging experience.
Modern Era (v14+ to present): Introduced Standalone components (removing NgModule requirements), Signals reactive primitive, and zoneless rendering.

slides.title: Core Architecture & Compiler
slides.subtitle: How Angular Applications Compiles and Operates Under the Hood

slides.title: Angular Workspace Compilation Flow
slides.content:
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ         Angular TypeScript Source Code        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      (Components, Templates, CSS, Directives) в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        в”Ӯ
                        в–ј  Angular CLI (ng build)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ            Ivy Compiler (AOT Engine)          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  - Parses HTML templates                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  - Type-checks templates against component TS в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  - Generates optimized JavaScript DOM code    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        в”Ӯ
                        в–ј  Tree-Shaking (Webpack/Esbuild)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ      (Main.js, Polyfills.js, Styles.css)      в”Ӯ

note.text: Ahead-of-Time (AOT) Ivy compiler validates templates during build time, preventing runtime errors in templates.

slides.title: Angular CLI: The Developer's Swiss Army Knife

box.title: рҹӣ пёҸ Command-Line Utilities
list: ng new [name] - Scaffolds a complete workspace with tests, styles, and routing configuration.
ng generate [type] [name] - Creates components, services, directives, guards, and pipes following style guides.
ng serve - Launches a fast, local development server with Hot Module Replacement (HMR).
ng build - Compiles the app into output files in the dist/ directory with optimizations.

slides.title: The Root Application Bootstrapping
slides.imageAlt: Code on a monitor representing JavaScript orchestration
slides.caption: The main entry point: bootstrapApplication
slides.content:
рҹ”Ҙ Standalone Bootstrapping

Modern Angular applications bypass standard NgModule definitions and bootstrap directly using a root Component.

  • вҡЎ main.ts - The entry point. Imports bootstrapApplication and configures core providers.
  • рҹ“Ӯ app.config.ts - Holds app-wide configurations like routers, HTTP client, and state services.
  • рҹҢҗ index.html - Contains the root element (e.g. <app-root>) which is replaced by Angular.

slides.title: Standalone Components & Anatomy
slides.subtitle: Building Modern Angular Applications without NgModules

slides.title: The Paradigm Shift: Standalone vs NgModule

box.title: рҹ’Ў Why did Angular introduce Standalone?
list: Before Standalone: Every component had to be declared in an NgModule, which managed imports, exports, and providers. This created heavy boilerplate and high learning curves.
With Standalone: Components directly declare their dependencies (components, directives, pipes) inside their imports array.
Modular: Reusability is simple. Just import the component where needed; no shared modules required.

slides.title: Anatomy of a Standalone Component
slides.imageAlt: Abstract illustration of puzzle blocks representing components
slides.caption: Metadata and logic unified in one file
slides.content:
вңҚпёҸ Component Anatomy

import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common';

@Component({
  selector: 'app-user-profile',
  standalone: true,
  imports: [CommonModule],
  templateUrl: './profile.component.html',
  styleUrls: ['./profile.component.css']
})
export class UserProfileComponent {
  username = 'Aravind';
}

slides.title: Component Metadata Config
leftPoints: рҹҸ·пёҸ selector: CSS selector that tells Angular where to instantiate the component.
рҹҸЎ template / templateUrl: Inline or external HTML structure defining the view.
рҹҺЁ styles / styleUrls: Scope-encapsulated styles for UI components.
rightPoints: рҹ§ұ standalone: true: Tells Ivy this component does not belong to any NgModule.
рҹ“Ұ imports: List of other components, directives, and pipes used in this component's template.
рҹ’ү providers: Define localized services injected for this component subtree.

note.text: Angular uses View Encapsulation (shadow DOM simulation) to prevent component CSS from leaking into other areas.

slides.title: Template Syntax & Directives
slides.subtitle: HTML Templating, Data Binding, and Structural Control Flows

box.title: рҹ”— Connecting Component Data to HTML Templates
list: Interpolation: {{ value }}. Inserts dynamic text into markup.
Property Binding: [src]="imageUrl". Binds component state variables to DOM element properties.
Event Binding: (click)="onSave()". Listens for user interactions and runs component code.
Two-Way Data Binding: [(ngModel)]="username". Synchronizes component variable and form input state in both directions.

slides.title: New Control Flow vs. Old Directives
leftPoints: *ngIf="isLoggedIn" requires importing CommonModule.
Verbose syntax using helper tags like <ng-template>.
Requires *ngFor="let item of list" to iterate arrays.
Uses [ngSwitch] for switch cases.
rightPoints: Built directly into the compiler: no imports required.
Clean JS-like syntax: @if (isLoggedIn) { ... } @else { ... }.
Extremely fast iterations: @for (item of list; track item.id) { ... }.
Mandatory track clause boosts DOM render performance.

note.text: The new @for block performs significantly faster than *ngFor and has built-in @empty blocks when arrays are empty.

slides.title: Attribute and Custom Directives
slides.caption: Extending element behavior using @Directive
slides.content:
вҡЎ Directives in Angular

Directives allow developers to attach custom behavior, modify classes/styles, or manipulate structure on host elements.

  • рҹҺЁ ngClass & ngStyle - Conditional CSS classes and inline styling.
  • рҹ”§ Custom Attribute Directives - Listening to mouse hover and dynamically modifying element styles.
  • рҹ§¬ Structure Directives - Adding or removing HTML elements programmatically.

slides.title: Pipes & Data Transformation
slides.subtitle: Formatting and Transforming Template Data on the Fly

box.title: рҹҡ° Streamlining Data Formatting directly in templates:
list: DatePipe: {{ user.joined | date:'shortDate' }}. Formats raw timestamp strings.
CurrencyPipe: {{ price | currency:'USD' }}. Renders currency symbols and decimas.
JsonPipe: <pre>{{ user | json }}</pre>. Prints debug object structures as strings.
AsyncPipe: {{ items$ | async }}. Automatically subscribes to Observables and unsubscribes when destroyed.

slides.title: Creating Custom Pipes
slides.caption: The transform method: PipeTransform interface
slides.content:
рҹҺЁ Custom Pipe Anatomy

import { Pipe, PipeTransform } from '@angular/core';

@Pipe({
  name: 'exponential',
  standalone: true
})
export class ExponentialPipe implements PipeTransform {
  transform(value: number, exponent = 1): number {
    return Math.pow(value, exponent);
  }
}

slides.title: Pure vs. Impure Pipes
leftPoints: Executed only when input reference changes.
Cached: Returns prior computed values if inputs remain identical.
Extremely fast and optimized for rendering loop performance.
Must use immutable state patterns to trigger re-computation.
rightPoints: Executed during every application change detection cycle.
No caching: Recalculates values on every mouse move or keystroke.
Can seriously degrade scrolling and interaction performance.
Necessary for dynamic asynchronous calls without standard dependencies.

note.text: Keep custom pipes Pure by default. If you need dynamic updates, rely on Services or Signals instead of Impure pipes.

slides.title: Dependency Injection & Services
slides.subtitle: Decoupling Logic with Angular's Hierarchical DI Engine

slides.title: Hierarchical Injector Lookup
slides.content:
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ               Platform Injector               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         (Global browser-level instances)       в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–Ів”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        в”Ӯ Parent Lookups
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                 Root Injector                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ        (AppConfig: `providedIn: 'root'`)       в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–Ів”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        в”Ӯ Parent Lookups
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ            Route / Element Injector           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    (Lazy Route definition or @Component)      в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–Ів”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        в”Ӯ DI Token Request
                 [ Component ]

note.text: Angular DI walks up the injector tree to resolve tokens. If a token is not found, it throws a No Provider Found error.

box.title: рҹ§¬ Creating Reusable Business Logic Classes
list: @Injectable Decorator: Marks a class as available for dependency injection.
providedIn: 'root': Declares service as a singleton at the app-wide level. Allows optimal compiler code tree-shaking.
Constructor Injection: The traditional way to declare dependencies inside class headers.
inject() Function (v14+): Modern functional API to query tokens outside constructor boundaries.

slides.title: inject() Function vs Constructor Injection
slides.caption: Choosing modern functional APIs
slides.content:
рҹ’ү Modern DI Syntax

// Traditional Constructor style
export class UserService {
  constructor(private http: HttpClient) {}
}

// Modern inject() style
export class UserService {
  private http = inject(HttpClient);
}

slides.title: Reactive Programming & RxJS
slides.subtitle: Asynchronous Streams, Reactive Programming, and Memory Management

box.title: рҹҢҠ Asynchronous Flow Concepts
list: Observable: A stream of values that can be observed over time.
Observer: A consumer that listens to values, error states, or completion triggers of an observable.
Subscription: Represents the execution context of an observable; must be managed to prevent memory leaks.
Subject / BehaviorSubject: Special Observables that allow multicasting values to multiple listeners (BehaviorSubject holds initial state).

slides.title: Observable Pipeline Transformation
slides.content:
Source Data Stream:  ---(1)-------(2)-------(3)--------->
                         в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
                       pipe(map(x => x * 10))
                         в–ј         в–ј         в–ј
Mapped Stream:       ---(10)------(20)------(30)-------->
                         в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
                       pipe(filter(x => x > 15))
                         в–ј         в–ј         в–ј
Filtered Stream:     ------------(20)------(30)-------->
                                   в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
                           subscribe(data => print)
                                   в–ј         в–ј
Console Output:                  "20"      "30"

note.text: RxJS pipelines transform raw events into refined state streams using composable, pure mathematical operators.

slides.title: Preventing Memory Leaks in RxJS
slides.caption: Managing subscriptions proactively
slides.content:
рҹӣЎпёҸ Subscription Safety

Failing to unsubscribe from global streams leaves components resident in browser memory, leading to heavy leaks.

  • рҹ”„ Async Pipe - Let Angular automatically subscribe/unsubscribe in HTML templates.
  • рҹӣ‘ takeUntilDestroyed - Modern operator that unsubscribes automatically when component destroys.
  • рҹ§© take(1) / first() - Completes the stream immediately after receiving the first payload.

slides.title: Common RxJS Operators
leftPoints: рҹ”„ map: Applies a transformation function to each emitted value.
рҹ§№ filter: Allows only matching values through to observers.
вҸі debounceTime: Ignores events until a specific quiet time has passed (great for search inputs).
rightPoints: вҡЎ switchMap: Cancels previous HTTP request and maps to new observable (avoids race conditions).
рҹ“Ҙ mergeMap: Fires multiple parallel requests simultaneously without cancelling any.
рҹ§ұ concatMap: Executes calls sequentially in order, waiting for prior streams to finish.

note.text: Use switchMap for search inputs and auto-completes to guarantee stale server responses are discarded.

slides.title: Angular Signals
slides.subtitle: Understanding the New Fine-Grained Reactive Primitives

slides.title: What are Angular Signals?
slides.imageAlt: Fibers showing connection and quick communication
slides.caption: Direct dependency tracking at the variable level
slides.content:
вҡЎ The New Reactive Core

Signals track where and when state is used, allowing Angular to trigger updates at a micro-level rather than checking entire DOM branches.

  • рҹҺҜ Fine-Grained Updates - Direct, precise DOM updates.
  • рҹ”— Auto-Dependency Tracking - Automatically updates dependent state.
  • вҡЎ Zoneless Ready - Allows compiling apps without Zone.js.

slides.title: Signals Reactive Dependency Graph
slides.content:
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ            Writable Signal (count)             в”Ӯ <в”Җв”Җв”Җ count.set(2)
в”Ӯ              (Holds core value)               в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        в”Ӯ
                        в–ј (Auto-tracks reads)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ         Computed Signal (doubleCount)         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    (Derived value: computed(() => count() * 2))в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        в”Ӯ
                        в–ј (Fires reactive execution)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ               Effect (Logging)                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ        (effect(() => console.log(double())))  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

note.text: Unlike RxJS, Signals do not require manual subscriptions. Dependencies are calculated automatically during function execution.

box.title: вңҚпёҸ Basic Signal Operations
list: Writable Signal: const count = signal(0);. Declares mutable variables.
Reading Values: count(). Evaluates value using function invocation syntax.
Mutating State: count.set(10) or count.update(v => v + 1).
Computed: computed(() => x() + y()). Declares read-only computed derivations that cache values.

slides.title: Signals vs. RxJS Observables
leftPoints: Synchronous state reading (no async await or pipelines).
Glitch-free (no transient invalid states/intermediate renders).
No subscriptions to unsubscribe or manage.
Ideal for UI state, component data, and DOM rendering.
rightPoints: Asynchronous events over time.
Requires pipeline execution operators (mapping, debouncing).
Requires careful subscription cleanup.
Ideal for HTTP streams, WebSockets, and complex time-based events.

note.text: Angular incorporates both: Signals handle local/UI state, and RxJS handles asynchronous network/event streams.

slides.title: Routing & Navigation
slides.subtitle: Configuring Single-Page Application Navigation & Route Guards

box.title: рҹ—әпёҸ Defining URL Mappings
list: Route Definition: Array mapping path strings to component exports.
Router Outlet: <router-outlet></router-outlet> component placeholder where routing displays matching components.
Router Link: routerLink="/users". Client-side navigation without full browser refresh.
Lazy Loading: loadComponent: () => import('./profile.component'). Bundles page code separately.

slides.title: Securing Routes with Guards
slides.caption: Functional guards: modern return checking
slides.content:
рҹӣЎпёҸ Functional Route Guards

Route guards intercept navigation attempts to perform checks like authentication or dirty-state check before rendering components.

  • рҹ”’ CanActivate - Checks if users can access pages (e.g. checks authorization token).
  • рҹ“Ұ Resolve - Fetches server data before navigating to avoid empty screen loading.
  • рҹҡӘ CanDeactivate - Warns users before leaving dirty unsaved forms.

slides.title: Route Configuration Syntax
leftPoints: Loads component immediately inside main bundle.
Simple declarations: component: HomeComponent.
Optimal for primary landing views.
rightPoints: Splits code into async chunks.
Dynamic imports: loadComponent: () => import(...).
Drastically reduces main bundle download sizes.

note.text: Combined with standalone components, lazy loading routes isolates component dependencies into separate files.

slides.title: Forms & Validation
slides.subtitle: Handling User Input with Reactive and Template-driven Systems

slides.title: Reactive vs. Template-driven Forms
leftPoints: State managed inside HTML template using [(ngModel)].
Asynchronous, harder to unit test.
Implicit, template-based validation attributes.
Good for simple, basic forms (e.g. login input).
rightPoints: Form structure declared explicitly inside TypeScript file.
Synchronous, easily tested via mock data structures.
Reactive validation: dynamic validator additions.
Good for complex enterprise workflows (e.g. multi-step forms).

note.text: For enterprise web apps, Reactive forms are preferred due to type-safety, testability, and reactive flow control.

slides.title: Declaring Reactive Forms
slides.caption: Using FormBuilder to group inputs
slides.content:
рҹ“қ Reactive Form Controls

import { FormBuilder, Validators } from '@angular/forms';

export class FormComponent {
  private fb = inject(FormBuilder);
  
  userForm = this.fb.group({
    email: ['', [Validators.required, Validators.email]],
    password: ['', [Validators.required, Validators.minLength(6)]]
  });
}

box.title: рҹӣЎпёҸ Validating User Inputs
list: Built-in Validators: Validators.required, Validators.pattern(), Validators.min().
Form Controls Status: Inspect properties like form.valid, form.invalid, form.dirty, form.touched.
Custom Synchronous Validators: Functions that evaluate form values and return error objects or null.
Async Validators: Evaluate backend responses (e.g. checking email uniqueness) returning Observables/Promises.

slides.title: State Management
slides.subtitle: Handling State with Shared Services, RxJS, and Signal Store

slides.title: State Management Strategies
leftPoints: Injectable Service holds state variables directly.
Exposes Observables or Signals to components.
Extremely lightweight, no third-party package dependencies.
Best for small to medium apps and localized component state.
rightPoints: Redux architecture: unidirectional data flow.
Store, Actions, Reducers, Effects, and Selectors.
Comprehensive state logging and DevTools support.
Best for complex enterprise apps with massive state interactions.

note.text: With the release of Angular Signals, many developers use lightweight Signal-based state services over bulky NgRx boilerplate.

slides.title: NgRx Redux State Lifecycle Flow
slides.content:
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                   Component                   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–Ів”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
        в”Ӯ Dispatches Action             в”Ӯ Subscribes Selectors
        в–ј                               в”Ӯ
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ               в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ    Action     в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ     Store     в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ               в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–Ів”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
        в”Ӯ Triggers                      в”Ӯ Mutates state
        в–ј                               в”Ӯ
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ               в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ    Effects    в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ   Reducers    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ (HTTP Calls)  в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ (Pure Funcs)  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ               в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

note.text: NgRx strictly isolates async side-effects in Effects, ensuring that Reducers remain pure functions that are easy to test.

box.title: вҡЎ Signal-based Store Patterns
list: NgRx SignalStore: Lightweight, opinionated state management utility built for Signals.
Functional Mixins: Allows composing state, computed selectors, and methods cleanly.
No Boilerplate: No complex actions or reducers required to perform simple mutations.
Highly Performant: Glitch-free, fine-grained UI updates with clean reactive logic.

slides.title: Testing, Optimization & Deployment
slides.subtitle: Testing Components, Optimizing Change Detection, and Building for SSR

box.title: рҹ§Ә Test Scaffolding and Utilities
list: TestBed: Main API that configures compilation of test modules and components.
ComponentFixture: Handles inspecting component DOM nodes and triggering change detection manually.
Mocking Services: Provide fake implementations to decouple API network calls from unit tests.
Jasmine & Karma / Jest: Standard frameworks to run assertions and generate test reports.

slides.title: Optimizing Change Detection
leftPoints: Checks every component in tree from root on any event.
Relies on Zone.js monkeys to capture browser events.
Requires zero developer configuration to work.
Can slow down performance in massive component trees.
rightPoints: Checks component only if Inputs reference changes.
Skips checking entire subtrees if inputs remain identical.
Requires immutable state operations.
Increases rendering performance; mandatory for large tables/lists.

note.text: OnPush strategy combined with Signals enables Angular applications to achieve near-instantaneous render updates.

slides.title: Server-Side Rendering (SSR) & Pre-rendering
slides.imageAlt: Data center racks representing SSR servers
slides.caption: Hydration: activating static client-side views
slides.content:
рҹҢҗ Server-Side Capabilities

Angular provides built-in SSR features using Express servers to pre-render static HTML elements before transferring code to clients.

  • рҹ“Ҳ SEO Optimization - Returns fully readable HTML to search engine bots.
  • рҹҸҺпёҸ Fast First Paint - Immediate content display.
  • рҹ’§ Client Hydration - Smart activation of elements without refreshing layouts.

slides.title: Client Hydration Lifecycle Flow
slides.content:
Client Request  в”Җв”Җв–ә [ Node Express Server ]
                           в”Ӯ
                           в–ј Pre-renders static HTML
Client Browser  в—„в”Җв”Җ [ Raw HTML + Inline Styles ]
   (Renders immediately, not interactive yet)
                           в”Ӯ
                           в–ј Downloads JS Bundles
Client Browser  в”Җв”Җв–ә [ Hydration / Bootstrapping ]
   (Attaches event listeners, updates signals)
                           в”Ӯ
                           в–ј
                      [ Fully Interactive App ]

note.text: Angular's modern hydration system matches DOM trees without destroying and recreating nodes, eliminating screen flickers.

slides.title: Knowledge Check
slides.subtitle: Test Your Understanding of Modern Angular Architecture and Reactivity

slides.question: Which metadata property is required to make a component standalone in Angular?
slides.explanation: To define a component as Standalone (bypassing NgModules), you must set 'standalone: true' in the @Component metadata.

slides.question: How do Angular Signals determine dependency tracking?
options: Manual declaration of dependencies inside dependency arrays.
Automatic tracking during function evaluation/read calls.
Declaring imports in the component's imports metadata.
slides.explanation: Angular Signals automatically track all signals read during the execution of computed functions or effects: no dependency arrays required.

slides.question: Which RxJS flattening operator is best suited for search inputs to prevent race conditions?
slides.explanation: switchMap is best for searches because it cancels the current/pending HTTP stream when a new search string is typed, preventing race conditions.

slides.title: Thank You!
slides.message:
Thank you for attending the Angular Masterclass!
You can find more resources and examples in our repository.
